cells.text: See Drive Parry. Perfect Parrying has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack. Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 11 frames of recovery. After a Perfect Parry, all Super Gauge gain is reduced by 20% and Drive Gauge gain/damage is reduced by 50% for the entire punish combo.

After a Perfect Parry, all Super Gauge gain is reduced by 20% and Drive Gauge gain/damage is reduced by 50% for the entire punish combo.

cells.text: See Drive Rush; 1-bar version performed out of a Parry; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation (if performed immediately after a successful Parry, only 1+8 startup); the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties; Distance: 0.375 (min/throw), 1.502 (min/block), 3.083 (max/final DR frame)

cells.html: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; cannot steer the attack forward like the other Monoid attacks; knocks down on both grounded and airborne opponents; input has no kara-cancel leniency (must hit HP+HK on the exact same frame); Modern Controls: Sp

cells.text: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; can hold forward to steer and increase the attack's range; knocks down on grounded opponents and resets airborne opponents; gains a second hitbox on active frames 3-6 that can hit cross-up; Modern Controls: j.X
cells.html: Requires summoning the Monoid with Ingrid's Back Taunt; can hold forward to steer and increase the attack's range; knocks down on grounded opponents and resets airborne opponents; gains a second hitbox on active frames 3-6 that can hit cross-up; Modern Controls: j.X

cells.text: Slow 3-bar Super with low damage relative to its meter cost; unsafe on hit or block; on hit, Ingrid returns and high-fives the Monoid (can transform into Shin Ingrid using the LP~LP~4~LK~HK input before the high five, becoming much more unsafe on hit); Modern Controls: 214236H
cells.html: Slow 3-bar Super with low damage relative to its meter cost; unsafe on hit or block; on hit, Ingrid returns and high-fives the Monoid (can transform into Shin Ingrid using the LP~LP~4~LK~HK input before the high five, becoming much more unsafe on hit); Modern Controls: 214236H

cells.text: Can hold the button to spend up to 2 Sun Crests (+400 damage per crest but reduced oki); Ingrid's primary reversal while in Burnout
cells.html: Can hold the button to spend up to 2 Sun Crests (+400 damage per crest but reduced oki); Ingrid's primary reversal while in Burnout

cells.text: Hold the button for ? frames to spend 1 Sun Crest for 400 extra damage and a farther knockdown
cells.html: Hold the button for ? frames to spend 1 Sun Crest for 400 extra damage and a farther knockdown

cells.text: Hold the button for ? frames to spend 2 Sun Crests for 800 extra damage and a farther knockdown
cells.html: Hold the button for ? frames to spend 2 Sun Crests for 800 extra damage and a farther knockdown

cells.text: Fires beacon into the air that spawns 3 downward-hitting projectiles; button strength determines beacon position (Modern Controls: hold back/neutral/forward to control position); LP version appears in front of the opponent, MP version appears above the opponent, HP version appears behind the opponent (except at fullscreen distance); can hold the button and spend 1 Sun Crest for an enhanced version that summons 2 additional projectiles; projectile startup varies based on strength: LP 103f / MP 68f / HP 98f
cells.html: Fires beacon into the air that spawns 3 downward-hitting projectiles; button strength determines beacon position (Modern Controls: hold back/neutral/forward to control position); LP version appears in front of the opponent, MP version appears above the opponent, HP version appears behind the opponent (except at fullscreen distance); can hold the button and spend 1 Sun Crest for an enhanced version that summons 2 additional projectiles; projectile startup varies based on strength: LP 103f / MP 68f / HP 98f

cells.text: Fires beacon into the air that spawns 4 sets of downward-hitting projectiles; button strength determines beacon position (Modern Controls: hold back/neutral/forward to control position); LP version appears in front of the opponent, MP version appears above the opponent, HP version appears behind the opponent (except at fullscreen distance); consumes 1 Sun Crest in exchange for the additional projectiles; projectile startup varies based on strength: LP 103f / MP 68f / HP 98f
cells.html: Fires beacon into the air that spawns 4 sets of downward-hitting projectiles; button strength determines beacon position (Modern Controls: hold back/neutral/forward to control position); LP version appears in front of the opponent, MP version appears above the opponent, HP version appears behind the opponent (except at fullscreen distance); consumes 1 Sun Crest in exchange for the additional projectiles; projectile startup varies based on strength: LP 103f / MP 68f / HP 98f

cells.text: Fires beacon into the air that spawns 5 sets of downward-hitting projectiles; button strength determines beacon position (Modern Controls: hold back/neutral/forward to control position); LP version appears in front of the opponent, MP version appears above the opponent, HP version appears behind the opponent (except at fullscreen distance); consumes 2 Sun Crests in exchange for the additional projectiles; projectile startup varies based on strength: LP 103f / MP 68f / HP 98f
cells.html: Fires beacon into the air that spawns 5 sets of downward-hitting projectiles; button strength determines beacon position (Modern Controls: hold back/neutral/forward to control position); LP version appears in front of the opponent, MP version appears above the opponent, HP version appears behind the opponent (except at fullscreen distance); consumes 2 Sun Crests in exchange for the additional projectiles; projectile startup varies based on strength: LP 103f / MP 68f / HP 98f

cells.text: Projectile hitbox that targets the opponent's position (can be avoided if opponent is ascending, like jumping after the Super freeze); cinematic time regenerates ~2.1 Drive bars for Ingrid; very slow startup makes it a poor reversal
cells.html: Projectile hitbox that targets the opponent's position (can be avoided if opponent is ascending, like jumping after the Super freeze); cinematic time regenerates ~2.1 Drive bars for Ingrid; very slow startup makes it a poor reversal

cells.text: Projectile hitbox that targets the opponent's position (can be avoided if opponent is ascending, like jumping after the Super freeze); available at 25% HP or below; cinematic time regenerates ~2.3 Drive bars for Ingrid; very slow startup makes it a poor reversal

cells.text: Shin Ingrid only; command grab Super; opponent cannot jump after the freeze; can be comboed into on grounded opponents; cinematic time regenerates ~1.6 Drive bars for Ingrid
